description: Epic 2: Build a concise partner pitch deck as a reveal.js HTML presentation

userStories.title: Expand agentic capability matrix with Finance and Sales functions
userStories.description: The capability_types and agent_capabilities tables are seeded with CI and Supply Chain data. Expand coverage to 2 more functions: (1) Manage Financial Resources (9.0) — add capabilities for: AP/AR monitoring, spend anomaly detection, budget variance analysis, journal entry generation, vendor payment execution (low/medium authority). (2) Market and Sell Products and Services (3.0) — add capabilities for: pricing anomaly monitoring, promotion performance analysis, order anomaly alerts, price adjustment execution (medium authority). Run `python3 business-agents/query.py capabilities` to verify output. No HTML or PPTX work in this story — DB only.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 6 new capabilities added for Finance (9.0) covering all 6 capability types
At least 6 new capabilities added for Sales/Marketing (3.0) covering all 6 capability types
Each capability has authority_level, data_inputs, data_outputs, authority_notes populated
`python3 business-agents/query.py capabilities` shows 28+ total capabilities across 4 functions

userStories.title: Define deck outline and narrative arc
userStories.description: Create a structured outline for the partner pitch deck. Save as office-skills/outputs/partner-pitch/outline.md. Define 11 slides with title, key message, and supporting points. Narrative: Title → Problem (Scale) → Problem (Why Automation Fails) → Vision → How It Works → What an Agent Is → Systems We Connect → First Pilot → Business Model → Why Us → Next Steps.
acceptanceCriteria: office-skills/outputs/partner-pitch/outline.md exists with full slide structure
Each slide has: title, 1-sentence key message, 3-5 bullet supporting points
Narrative arc flows logically from problem to call to action

userStories.title: Build Problem slides — the enterprise automation gap
userStories.description: Create the Problem section as 2 reveal.js slides inside office-skills/outputs/partner-pitch/index.html. Slide 1: Scale of manual work in enterprise (cost, errors, speed). Slide 2: Why existing automation fails — RPA brittleness, no reasoning, exception handling gaps vs. generic AI with no grounding. Use reveal.js via CDN. Strong visual headline per slide, minimal text, dark professional theme.
acceptanceCriteria: office-skills/outputs/partner-pitch/index.html exists with reveal.js loaded via CDN
2 problem slides with strong visual headlines and minimal bullet text
Consistent dark professional theme established

userStories.title: Build Solution slides — vision, approach, capability matrix, and agent explanation
userStories.description: Add 5 slides to index.html: (1) Vision: 'Agentic pilots built on your actual processes' — 4-step arc (Map → Score → Pilot → Scale). (2) How It Works: Process Map → Opportunity Matrix → Agent Pilot → Scale with brief description of each step. (3) Agentic Capability Matrix (Option B): a visual grid showing Business Functions as rows and the 6 Capability Types as columns (Monitor & Detect, Root Cause Analysis, Idea & Option Generation, Evaluate & Score, Execute & Act, Manage & Track). Each cell is color-coded by authority level: LOW=green (fully auto, reversible), MEDIUM=yellow (auto + notification), HIGH=orange (draft + human approves), EMPTY=grey. Pull data from `python3 business-agents/query.py capabilities` to populate real cells. (4) What an Agent Is: agent loop diagram (Monitor → Reason → Act → Report → Escalate), comparison to RPA and chatbots. (5) Systems We Connect: show SAP, Salesforce, MES, CRM, S&OP, POS — pull names from business-agents DB using python3 business-agents/query.py systems.
acceptanceCriteria: 5 slides added to index.html in the solution section
Capability matrix slide shows a grid with functions as rows and 6 capability type columns
Matrix cells color-coded: green=LOW, yellow=MED, orange=HIGH, grey=empty
Matrix populated with real data from business_agents.db (at minimum CI and Supply Chain rows)
Authority level legend included on the matrix slide
Agent loop clearly differentiated from RPA

userStories.title: Build First Pilot slide — demand forecasting agent
userStories.description: Add 1-2 slides to index.html showing the concrete demand forecasting pilot. Run `python3 business-agents/query.py opportunities CG` to pull process 4.1.1.1 data. Show: workflow (SAP + POS → agent → forecast → S&OP), systems connected, business value (time saved, accuracy improvement). Make data visual with a simple left-to-right flow diagram using HTML/CSS.
acceptanceCriteria: Slides reference real data from business_agents.db (process name, systems, action type)
Agent workflow clearly visualized as a horizontal flow
Quantified business value included (even if estimated ranges)

userStories.title: Build Business Model slide — three-phase engagement
userStories.description: Add 1 slide to index.html showing the three-phase engagement model: Discovery (2-3 weeks, process mapping + opportunity scoring, deliverable: priority matrix) → Pilot (4-8 weeks, one agent live, deliverable: working agent) → Expand (ongoing, agent library + retainer, deliverable: automation roadmap). Visualize as a horizontal timeline with phase cards using HTML/CSS flexbox.
acceptanceCriteria: Three phases clearly shown with timeframes and deliverables
Revenue model implied per phase
Clean, professional timeline or card layout using CSS only

userStories.title: Build Why Us and Next Steps slides
userStories.description: Add 2 final slides to index.html. Slide 1 (Why Us): 4 differentiators as cards — (1) Process-first not tech-first, (2) Pre-built from APQC PCF taxonomy (500+ processes mapped), (3) Faster pilot via pre-scored opportunity matrices, (4) Works with existing systems — no rip and replace. Slide 2 (Next Steps): 3-step CTA (30-min intro call → Process walkthrough → Pilot scoping) plus contact info placeholder.
acceptanceCriteria: 4 differentiators clearly stated as visual cards
Implicitly positions against DIY AI, generic consulting, RPA
Next steps slide has 3-step CTA and contact placeholder
Confident, concise tone throughout

userStories.title: Polish and finalize the HTML presentation
userStories.description: Review the complete reveal.js presentation in office-skills/outputs/partner-pitch/index.html. Apply final polish: (1) Consistent typography — use a single Google Font (e.g. Inter or DM Sans) via CDN. (2) Color palette: dark background (#0f1117 or similar), accent color (#6366f1 or brand color). (3) Check all slides for text overflow, alignment, readability. (4) Add a slide number counter. (5) Ensure keyboard navigation and fullscreen work. (6) Verify all slides are present and flow correctly: Title → Problem (2) → Vision → Approach → Agent → Systems → Pilot → Business Model → Why Us → Next Steps.
acceptanceCriteria: office-skills/outputs/partner-pitch/index.html is a complete, polished presentation
Consistent font, color palette, and spacing across all slides
No text overflow or layout issues on any slide
Slide counter visible, keyboard navigation works
All 11 slides present in correct narrative order
